PLF Core:
Your Path Through PLF
!
Executive Summary
This video is all about how PLF fits into your life and business. First of all,
business has changed. These days it’s all about delivering value first and
you need to understand this.
The first thing you’ll learn and accomplish with PLF is how to launch, and
then you learn how to do it over and over again. I’m actually using PLF
within the course itself. Sequencing, triggers, stories - they’re all in there.
After that you’ll learn how to build these lessons into your blog posts,
products, emails, etc. There’s now more transparency, trust, and
relationship building present in successful businesses. 10-15 years ago we
didn’t have this, but it’s required now.
When you do your launch the spotlight will be focused on you, your
business, and your product. PLF aligns your product with what people want
and how our brains actually work - this will set you up for success.
And just to be clear - you may already be an expert, or may not want to be
one at all…both ways work.
John Gallagher started simply. He created an educational board game for
kids about how to use plants as medicine. He began with a tiny newsletter
!

list and sold just 12 games. After PLF, and a proper product launch, he sold
670 games. Now he’s sold 50,000 games, and that’s just the tip of the
iceberg!
Since then he’s developed online courses, expanded the membership site,
and he’s not a guru. His role is having expertise in marketing for his niche,
and he’s built up a great business now.
Another example: Susan Garrett teaches dog agility training, a tiny little
niche and competitive sport. She had a goal to pay off the cost of PLF
before the credit card bill came in, and she did. First she came out with an
ebook, and now she’s established herself as the #1 guru within her market.
Or what about my own story? When I first started in the stock market niche,
I had zero expertise or credentials. I was simply the front-man for the
operation, but had someone in the background with the expertise.
With PLF, I started with a simple Seed Launch and have worked my way up
to now even being a New York Times #1 bestselling author.
The examples I mentioned above all started by stacking the results and
making incremental change. PLF shows you how to influence people and
get them to take action, and this is how you grow your business.
This is also all about becoming more attractive to your audience, because
this way you can get what you want much more easily. Once you get there,
business becomes much easier.
!

It’s your choice: you can make money, or you can save the world. Most PLF
Owners start by just wanting to grow their business, but eventually they
grow as people.
!
The Entrepreneur’s Hierarchy of Needs
From bottom to top in order of evolution:
1. Money
2. Time
3. Relationship
4. Purpose
You can have all of these needs met if you take PLF, learn it, embody it,
and run with it.
Finally, leave a comment on this video, and let me know: where are you in
your journey? Or just say “I’m here Jeff, and I’m ready!”.
!
